steps.title: Find angle UTW using supplementary angles
steps.content:
Since point T lies on segment SW, the rays TS and TW point in opposite directions along line SW. Angles STU and UTW are supplementary.
$$\text{angle } STU + \text{angle } UTW = 180^{\circ}$$
$$74^{\circ} + \text{angle } UTW = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\text{angle } UTW = 106^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Use co-interior angles from TU ∥ VW
steps.content:
Since TU is parallel to VW, and line segment TW acts as a transversal, the co-interior angles sum to $180^{\circ}$.
$$\text{angle } UTW + \text{angle } TWV = 180^{\circ}$$
$$106^{\circ} + \text{angle } TWV = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\text{angle } TWV = 74^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Identify p and r from the figure using alternate interior angles
steps.content:
The figure labels $p = \angle UTW$ (the angle at $T$ between ray $TU$ and ray $TW$, already found to be $106^{\circ}$) and $r = \angle UTV$ (the angle at $T$ between ray $TU$ and ray $TV$).

Since $TU \parallel VW$, with transversal $TV$, alternate interior angles are equal:
$$r = \angle UTV = \angle TVW = 26^{\circ}$$
(The figure gives $\angle TVW = 26^{\circ}$.)
